paper.title: A binary system in the S cluster close to the supermassive black hole Sagittarius A*

review.summary: Paper reports detection of a binary system D9 in the S cluster near Sgr A*. Internal tables include best-fit Keplerian/SED parameters, binary comparison, flux density tests, extinction comparison, GC binary overview, RV epoch data (2005-2022), and observation logs. External resources are code availability URLs for HYPERION, PARSEC, Exo-Stricker, emcee, and ESO pipeline. No HVS/unbound candidates; this is a Galactic center binary study.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Best-fit orbital and stellar parameters of the D9 binary system, combining Keplerian orbit around Sgr A*, binary orbit parameters, and SED-derived stellar properties.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Best-fit parameters of the D9 system.} We list the orbital parameters for the binary of D9 together with the motion of the system around Sgr~A*. In addition, the best-fit stellar properties based on the SED fitter are included. The uncertainties of the binary parameter and the radiation transfer model are based on the reduced $\chi^2$. For the Keplerian elements, we use MCMC simulations to estimate the uncertainty range. {Since the inclination of the secondary is assumed to be $\mathrm{i}_{\rm D9b}\,=\,90^{\circ}$, no uncertainty for $m \sin (\mathrm{i}_{\rm D9b})\,=\,0.73$~M$_{\odot}$ is given.}
source_refs.label: tab:d9_parameter

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Physical parameter name (orbital, stellar, or model)

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Best-fit or fixed value of the parameter

internal_tables.evidence: {\bf Best-fit parameters of the D9 system.} We list the orbital parameters for the binary of D9 together with the motion of the system around Sgr~A*. In addition, the best-fit stellar properties based on the SED fitter are included. The uncertainties of the binary parameter and the radiation transfer model are based on the reduced $\chi^2$. For the Keplerian elements, we use MCMC simulations to estimate the uncertainty range. {Since the inclination of the secondary is assumed to be $\mathrm{i}_{\rm D9b}\,=\,90^{\circ}$, no uncertainty for $m \sin (\mathrm{i}_{\rm D9b})\,=\,0.73$~M$_{\odot}$ is given.}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Comparison between best-fit Keplerian approximation and MCMC simulations for D9 orbital parameters around Sgr A*.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Comparison of best-fit Keplerian approximation and MCMC simulations.} Since the standard deviation does not satisfactorily reflect the astrometric precision that can be achieved with SINFONI, we will use the standard deviation of the combined MCMC posteriors. {These orbital elements are related to the outer binary system D9-Sgr~A*.} We refer to \cite{peissker2021c} for a detailed explanation of the background fluctuations of the SINFONI data.
source_refs.label: tab:d9_kepler_comp

columns.name: Standard deviation
columns.meaning: Standard deviation from combined MCMC posteriors

internal_tables.evidence: {\bf Comparison of best-fit Keplerian approximation and MCMC simulations.} Since the standard deviation does not satisfactorily reflect the astrometric precision that can be achieved with SINFONI, we will use the standard deviation of the combined MCMC posteriors. {These orbital elements are related to the outer binary system D9-Sgr~A*.} We refer to \cite{peissker2021c} for a detailed explanation of the background fluctuations of the SINFONI data.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Impact of different visual extinction values on SED-derived stellar radius, temperature, disk mass, and envelope size of D9.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Comparison of the impact of different extinction values.} For all three inspected extinction values, the stellar radius and temperature of D9 are comparable inside the uncertainties calculated by the radiative transfer model.
source_refs.label: tab:extinction_comp

columns.name: Radius
columns.meaning: Stellar radius from radiative transfer model

columns.name: Envelope size
columns.meaning: Dust envelope size from radiative transfer model

internal_tables.evidence: {\bf Comparison of the impact of different extinction values.} For all three inspected extinction values, the stellar radius and temperature of D9 are comparable inside the uncertainties calculated by the radiative transfer model.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Overview of known binaries in the Galactic center, with D9 as the only S-cluster member.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Binaries of the Galactic center.} In this list, D9 is the only S~cluster member.
source_refs.label: tab:binary_overview

internal_tables.evidence: {\bf Binaries of the Galactic center.} In this list, D9 is the only S~cluster member.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Radial velocity measurements from SINFONI observations of D9 Br-gamma emission line, 2005-2012.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Radial velocities measured from the SINFONI observations carried out between 2005-2012.} We list the epoch, the wavelength, the corresponding Full-Width-Half-Maximum, the LOS velocity, and the related uncertainty. Furthermore, we indicate the normalized RV v$_{\rm norm}$ and the final velocity v$_{\rm mod}$ where the Keplerian model for the secondary from Exo-Stricker is included. All velocities, except v$_{\rm mod}$, are estimated with respect to the Br$\gamma$ rest wavelength at $2.1661\,\mathrm{\mu m}$.
source_refs.label: tab:rv_values_1

columns.name: FWHM
columns.meaning: Full width at half maximum of the emission line

columns.name: RV
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity from line shift

columns.name: v_mod
columns.meaning: Model residual velocity after Keplerian subtraction

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Radial velocity measurements from SINFONI and ERIS observations of D9 Br-gamma emission line, 2015-2022.

source_refs.caption: {\bf Radial velocities measured from the SINFONI and ERIS observations carried out between 2015-2022.} Like Table \ref{tab:rv_values_1} and Table \ref{tab:rv_values_2}, we indicate all relevant observables that are used to derive the best-fit RV model displayed in the periodic pattern of D9. Here, v$_{\rm mod}$ indicates the final RV velocity. The data points from 2022 were observed with ERIS.
source_refs.label: tab:rv_values_3

external_resources.description: PARSEC stellar evolution tracks (v3.7) used for isochrone fitting.
HYPERION radiative transfer code (v1.4) used for SED generation.
emcee Python MCMC ensemble sampler package.
ESO data reduction pipeline for SINFONI/ERIS spectroscopy.
